name: account.mail
title: Account:Mail
description: The declaration to configure a Mail account.

payload.content: An email configuration defines an email account for a user.

payloadkeys.title: Account Name
payloadkeys.content: The name that apps show to the user for this mail account. If not present, the system generates a suitable default.

payloadkeys.title: User Identity Asset Reference
payloadkeys.content: The identifier of an asset declaration that contains the user identity for this account. Set the corresponding asset type to `UserIdentity`.

payloadkeys.title: Incoming Server Settings
payloadkeys.content: The settings for the incoming mail server for this account.

subkeys.title: Server Type
subkeys.content: The mail protocol this account uses.

subkeys.title: Server Host Name
subkeys.content: The host name for the incoming mail server.

subkeys.title: Server Port
subkeys.content: The port number for the incoming mail server.

subkeys.title: Server Authentication Method
subkeys.content: The authentication method for the incoming mail server.

subkeys.title: Authentication Credentials Asset Reference
subkeys.content:
The identifier of an asset declaration that contains the credentials for this account to authenticate with an incoming mail server. The corresponding asset must be of type `CredentialUserNameAndPassword`.

If the `AuthenticationMethod` is `None`, this field must be blank. Otherwise, the declaration must contain this field.

subkeys.title: IMAP Path Prefix
subkeys.content: The path prefix for the IMAP server. The system uses this only when `ServerType` is `IMAP`.

payloadkeys.title: Outgoing Server Settings
payloadkeys.content: The settings for the outgoing mail server for this account.

subkeys.title: Server Host Name
subkeys.content: The host name for the outgoing mail server.

subkeys.title: Server Port
subkeys.content: The port number for the outgoing mail server.

subkeys.title: Server Authentication Method
subkeys.content: The authentication method for the outgoing mail server.

subkeys.title: Authentication Credentials Asset Reference
subkeys.content:
The identifier of an asset declaration that contains the credentials for this account to authenticate with an outgoing mail server. The corresponding asset must be of type `CredentialUserNameAndPassword`.

If the `AuthenticationMethod` is `None`, this field must be blank. Otherwise, the declaration must contain this field.

subkeys.title: S/MIME Signing Settings
subkeys.content: Settings for S/MIME signing.

subkeys.title: Signing Enabled
subkeys.content: If `true`, the system enables S/MIME signing.

subkeys.title: S/MIME Signing Identity Asset Reference
subkeys.content: Specifies the identifier of an asset declaration containing the identity required for S/MIME signing of messages sent from this account.

subkeys.title: Signing User Overrideable
subkeys.content: If `true`, the user can turn S/MIME signing on or off in Settings.

subkeys.title: Signing Identity User Overrideable
subkeys.content: If `true`, the user can select an S/MIME signing identity in Settings.

subkeys.title: S/MIME Encryption Settings
subkeys.content: Settings for S/MIME encryption.

subkeys.title: Encryption By Default Enabled
subkeys.content: If `true`, the system enables S/MIME encryption by default, which the user can't override if `PerMessageSwitchEnabled` is `false`.

subkeys.title: S/MIME Encryption Identity Asset Reference
subkeys.content: Specifies the identifier of an asset declaration containing the identity required for S/MIME encryption. The system attaches the public certificate to outgoing mail to allow the user to receive encrypted mail. When the user sends encrypted mail, the system uses the public certificate to encrypt the copy of the mail in their Sent mailbox.

subkeys.title: Encryption By Default User Overrideable
subkeys.content: If `true`, the user can set the default value for S/MIME encryption to on or off in Settings.

subkeys.title: Encryption Identity User Overrideable
subkeys.content: If `true`, the user can select an S/MIME signing identity in Settings.

subkeys.title: Per Message Switch Enabled
subkeys.content: If `true`, the system enables the per-message encryption switch in the compose view.
